title: rinorea gazensis
intro: Rinorea gazensis, also known as the Gazelle plum or Wild plum, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of East Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark and leaves, which are used to treat various health conditions. The bark is rich in tannins and has been used to treat fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues, while the leaves have been used to treat malaria and other infectious diseases. Rinorea gazensis can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which have been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues.
